_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average price index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice set to 100 in 2015, currently stands at 132, indicating an approximate 32% increase over the period. This rise has been uneven across the components and largely driven by recent price changes. The index reflects global commodity prices and serves as a broad indicator of soft commodity trends related to common breakfast staples. The climb is notable but not uniform, with some items moving much more than others.
notes.movers: Coffee has seen the largest price increase, rising 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 22%, wheat at 7%, and sugar at 5%. These disparities highlight that the price shifts are not due to generalized inflation but rather specific supply-side factors including weather events and crop diseases. For example, hurricanes and citrus greening affect orange juice production, while frost and drought impact coffee-growing regions. These soft commodity markets tend to be thin, meaning smaller volumes can lead to significant price volatility.
notes.why: A few weather-sensitive crops with constrained supply have driven the headline index rise more than broad economic factors. Because these commodities are vulnerable to natural shocks, prices can remain elevated for extended periods until conditions normalize. This dynamic underscores the role of environmental risks in commodity pricing.
